perturbation.original_text: 12. Liquidated Damages. In case of unauthorized use or disclosure of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION,The DISCLOSING PARTY shall be entitled to liquidated damages in the amount of €10 000 (tenthousand Euro) for each such use or disclosure. Notwithstanding the right to liquidated damages,the DISCLOSING PARTY has the right to take any measures available and to claim and receivea higher amount of compensation if the DISCLOSING PARTY can prove that the actual damagesustained will exceed the amount of liquidated damages.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. Liquidated Damages. In case of unauthorized use or disclosure of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, The DISCLOSING PARTY shall be entitled to liquidated damages. Notwithstanding the right to liquidated damages, the DISCLOSING PARTY has the right to take any measures available and to claim and receive a higher amount of compensation if the DISCLOSING PARTY can prove that the actual damage sustained will exceed the amount of liquidated damages.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific amount of liquidated damages (€10,000), the contract now states the disclosing party is entitled to liquidated damages but does not define the amount. This creates an in-text contradiction because the clause still refers to 'the right to liquidated damages' as if the amount were known, creating uncertainty in enforcement. Parties now cannot be sure how much it will be, which will go against the section that refers to it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. Liquidated Damages. In case of unauthorized use or disclosure of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, The DISCLOSING PARTY shall be entitled to liquidated damages. Notwithstanding the right to liquidated damages, the DISCLOSING PARTY has the right to take any measures available and to claim and receive a higher amount of compensation if the DISCLOSING PARTY can prove that the actual damage sustained will exceed the amount of liquidated damages.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies the liquidated damages amount as €10,000. The changed text removes this specific amount, creating a contradiction with the contradicted text (which is the same as the changed text) that refers to 'the right to liquidated damages' as if a specific amount is known. This omission introduces uncertainty and makes the concept of liquidated damages less concrete and enforceable, thus creating an in-text contradiction as the clause still refers to 'the right to liquidated damages' as if the amount were known.

perturbation.original_text: 5. No Disclosure. The RECEIVING PARTY and its employees, officers, agents and affiliates shall holdthe CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION in confidence and take all reasonable steps to preserve theconfidential and proprietary nature of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, including, withoutlimitation: (i) refraining from disclosing the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to persons within itsorganization not having a reason to know, and all persons outside its organization unless theyhave a reason to know and are bound by fiduciary duties of confidentiality to the RECEIVING PARTY;and (ii) advising all of its employees, officers, agents and affiliates who gain access to theCONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION of its confidential and proprietary nature. The obligations of thisparagraph also apply to the fact of the existence of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, of thisAgreement, and the occurrence of all meetings and communications of the parties that involveCONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. In the event the RECEIVING PARTY receives in writing an opinion from itsoutside counsel that it must disclose certain information, prohibited by the terms herein, in orderto avoid committing a violation of law, the RECEIVING PARTY may disclose only that informationnecessary to avoid committing such violation of the law.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. No Disclosure. The RECEIVING PARTY and its employees, officers, agents and affiliates shall hold the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION in confidence and take all reasonable steps to preserve the confidential and proprietary nature of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, including, without limitation: (i) refraining from disclosing the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to persons within its organization not having a reason to know, and all persons outside its organization unless they have a reason to know and are bound by fiduciary duties of confidentiality to the RECEIVING PARTY; and (ii) advising all of its employees, officers, agents and affiliates who gain access to the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION of its confidential and proprietary nature. The obligations of this paragraph also apply to the fact of the existence of this Agreement, and the occurrence of all meetings and communications of the parties.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION', 'that involve CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' and 'In the event the RECEIVING PARTY receives in writing an opinion from its outside counsel that it must disclose certain information, prohibited by the terms herein, in order to avoid committing a violation of law, the RECEIVING PARTY may disclose only that information necessary to avoid committing such violation of the law.' a contradiction is created. The first part of the section states how important it is to keep the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION in confidence, however removing the end changes the initial meaning of the paragraph.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. No Disclosure. The RECEIVING PARTY and its employees, officers, agents and affiliates shall hold the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION in confidence and take all reasonable steps to preserve the confidential and proprietary nature of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, including, without limitation: (i) refraining from disclosing the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to persons within its organization not having a reason to know, and all persons outside its organization unless they have a reason to know and are bound by fiduciary duties of confidentiality to the RECEIVING PARTY; and (ii) advising all of its employees, officers, agents and affiliates who gain access to the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION of its confidential and proprietary nature. The obligations of this paragraph also apply to the fact of the existence of this Agreement, and the occurrence of all meetings and communications of the parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that the confidentiality applied to 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION', limiting the scope of the non-disclosure agreement. The changed text broadens the scope of the non-disclosure agreement by removing 'of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' and 'that involve CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION'. Additionally, the original text had a clause that allowed for disclosure if required by law, which was removed. This creates a direct contradiction because the changed text now implies that all meetings and communications, regardless of whether they involve confidential information, are subject to strict non-disclosure, and provides no exception for legally required disclosures. The contradicted text is a copy of the changed text, so this means that the modified section is in contradiction with itself now.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Return. All CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION supplied by the DISCLOSING PARTY hereunder and all copiesthereof, in whole or in part and on all media, shall be returned to the DISCLOSING PARTY by theRECEIVING PARTY within thirty (30) days upon demand by the DISCLOSING PARTY. All otherinformation and all copies thereof prepared by the RECEIVING PARTY based on the CONFIDENTIALINFORMATION, shall be either promptly destroyed or returned to the DISCLOSING PARTY upondemand, as determined by the DISCLOSING PARTY within its sole discretion.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Return. All CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION supplied by the DISCLOSING PARTY hereunder and all copies thereof, in whole or in part and on all media, shall be returned to the DISCLOSING PARTY by the RECEIVING PARTY within thirty (30) days upon demand by the DISCLOSING PARTY. All other information and all copies thereof prepared by the RECEIVING PARTY shall be either promptly destroyed or returned to the DISCLOSING PARTY upon demand, as determined by the DISCLOSING PARTY within its sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'based on the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION', the modified text creates ambiguity. It now states 'All other information and all copies thereof prepared by the RECEIVING PARTY shall be either promptly destroyed or returned'. This is too broad because it could imply that all information created by the receiving party, regardless of its connection to the confidential information must be destroyed or returned, creating an in-text contradiction and broadens the obligations significantly.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Return. All CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION supplied by the DISCLOSING PARTY hereunder and all copies thereof, in whole or in part and on all media, shall be returned to the DISCLOSING PARTY by the RECEIVING PARTY within thirty (30) days upon demand by the DISCLOSING PARTY. All other information and all copies thereof prepared by the RECEIVING PARTY shall be either promptly destroyed or returned to the DISCLOSING PARTY upon demand, as determined by the DISCLOSING PARTY within its sole discretion.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'All other information and all copies thereof prepared by the RECEIVING PARTY based on the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION...'. The changed text removes 'based on the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION', broadening the scope of what information must be destroyed or returned. This creates a contradiction because it implies that *all* information created by the receiving party, regardless of its connection to the confidential information, is subject to destruction or return, while the original text limits it to information based on confidential information. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, further highlighting the introduced contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10. Duration. This Agreement shall apply to any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION that may have beenprovided to the RECEIVING PARTY prior to or after the date hereof, and shall continue to govern thedelivery of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION until terminated by written notice from either party to theother, except that the obligations of the parties hereunder with regard to CONFIDENTIALINFORMATION disclosed prior to termination shall continue for a period for two (2) years thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Duration. This Agreement shall apply to any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION that may have been provided to the RECEIVING PARTY prior to or after the date hereof, and shall continue to govern the delivery of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION until terminated by written notice from either party to the other, except that the obligations of the parties hereunder shall continue for a period for two (2) years thereafter.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'with regard to CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION disclosed prior to termination', the modified text expands the scope of the obligations that continue for two years post-termination. Now, it's unclear whether *all* obligations under the agreement continue, not just those relating to previously disclosed confidential information. This is a contradiction from the original scope, since the original obligations are meant to refer to the obligations prior to the termination, and specifically the obligations related to confidental information, since is an NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Duration. This Agreement shall apply to any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION that may have been provided to the RECEIVING PARTY prior to or after the date hereof, and shall continue to govern the delivery of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION until terminated by written notice from either party to the other, except that the obligations of the parties hereunder shall continue for a period for two (2) years thereafter.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 10 omits the phrase 'with regard to CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION disclosed prior to termination.' This broadens the scope of obligations that continue for two years post-termination. The original text specifies that only obligations related to previously disclosed confidential information continue, making the modified text's scope unclear, encompassing potentially all obligations. This creates a contradiction because the original intent limits post-termination obligations specifically to confidential information, whereas the changed text could be interpreted as extending all contractual obligations for two years, thus creating a strong contradiction to the contradicted text because it is the same section.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Injunctive Relief. The RECEIVING PARTY understands and agrees that monetary damages will not besufficient to avoid or compensate for the unauthorized use or disclosure of the CONFIDENTIALINFORMATION and that injunctive relief would be appropriate to prevent any actual or threateneduse of disclosure of such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Injunctive Relief. The RECEIVING PARTY understands and agrees that monetary damages will not be sufficient to avoid or compensate for the unauthorized use or disclosure of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION and that injunctive relief would be appropriate to prevent any actual or threatened use of disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'of such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION', the modified text makes the injunctive relief section ambiguous. It's no longer explicitly stated that the injunctive relief is to prevent disclosure of the confidential information, creating a contradiction as the section now seems to imply injunctive relief for any disclosure, even if unrelated to the confidential information. This creates ambiguity, since from the beginning the NDA is related to CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, so this affects the essence of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Injunctive Relief. The RECEIVING PARTY understands and agrees that monetary damages will not be sufficient to avoid or compensate for the unauthorized use or disclosure of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION and that injunctive relief would be appropriate to prevent any actual or threatened use of disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 11, by removing 'of such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION', broadens the scope of injunctive relief beyond just the unauthorized use or disclosure of confidential information. The contradicted text, which is the same as the original text in this case, explicitly limits injunctive relief to the unauthorized use or disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. This omission creates a contradiction because the changed text now implies injunctive relief could be sought for any unauthorized use of disclosure, even if unrelated to confidential information, which is contrary to the original intent and scope of the NDA as a whole. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 15. Governing law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the lawsof Netherlands, excluding its rules for choice of law. Notwithstanding an action for injunction orother equitable remedy, all disputes relating to or arising out of or in connection with thisAgreement shall be finally settled by arbitration in accordance with the Rules of the ArbitrationInstitute of the Amsterdam Chamber of Commerce. The arbitration shall be held in theNetherlands, Amsterdam and the English language shall be used in the proceedings. Thearbitration decision shall be final and binding on the parties and enforceable in any court ofcompetent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. Governing law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the lawsof Netherlands, excluding its rules for choice of law. Notwithstanding an action for injunction orother equitable remedy, all disputes relating to or arising out of or in connection with thisAgreement shall be settled by in accordance with the Rules of the ArbitrationInstitute of the Amsterdam Chamber of Commerce. The arbitration shall be held in theNetherlands, Amsterdam and the English language shall be used in the proceedings. Thearbitration decision shall be final and binding on the parties and enforceable in any court ofcompetent jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'finally' before 'settled by arbitration', creates uncertainty about whether arbitration is the *exclusive* method of dispute resolution. This contradicts the intention of a standard arbitration clause, which is typically designed to be the final and binding method to resolve the dispute, thus implying that disputes could also be taken to court which is contradictory to the idea of an NDA, creating ambiguity around dispute resolution and contradicting the original intent of the arbitration clause. If not specified that is finally settled by arbitration, then it can be assumed it can be settled by any process.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15. Governing law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the lawsof Netherlands, excluding its rules for choice of law. Notwithstanding an action for injunction orother equitable remedy, all disputes relating to or arising out of or in connection with thisAgreement shall be settled by in accordance with the Rules of the ArbitrationInstitute of the Amsterdam Chamber of Commerce. The arbitration shall be held in theNetherlands, Amsterdam and the English language shall be used in the proceedings. Thearbitration decision shall be final and binding on the parties and enforceable in any court ofcompetent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that all disputes 'shall be finally settled by arbitration'. The changed text omits 'finally', creating ambiguity about whether arbitration is the exclusive method of dispute resolution. This contradicts the intention of a standard arbitration clause, which is typically designed to be the final and binding method to resolve the dispute, thus implying that disputes could also be taken to court. The contradicted text is the same location as the changed text, but the omission of 'finally' creates a direct conflict, making the enforceability of the clause questionable and thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.
